tank: Hold aggro on EVERYTHING before DPS opens up; use a focused opener on the kill target, then AoE threat.
Face bosses AWAY from the group so cleaves/breaths/frontal cones hit only you.
Pull at a controlled pace - wait for healer mana and don't chain packs the group can't handle.
Watch for adds that spawn mid-fight; pick them up immediately so they don't reach healers/casters.
Mark targets (skull = kill first, X = second) and CC sources so the group focuses correctly.
Keep an active mitigation/defensive ready for big hits; don't blow all cooldowns at once.
healer: Stay in range/line-of-sight of the whole group; reposition early, not after someone is already low.
Manage mana: let the tank's self-mitigation and DPS armor soak small hits, save big heals for spikes.
Pre-heal/HoT before known burst mechanics instead of reacting after the damage lands.
Triage: keep the tank alive first, then yourself, then DPS; a dead healer wipes the group.
Dispel/decurse what your class can when it actually matters (lethal debuffs, not cosmetic ones).
Call for a pull stop when low on mana rather than risking a wipe.
dps: Never pull off the tank - hold your opener a couple of GCDs and watch the threat meter (Omen).
Stand BEHIND the boss (melee) or at max range spread (ranged) to avoid cleaves and frontal attacks.
Always swap to priority adds/spawns when called (skull first), then back to the boss.
Move out of ground effects (fire/void/poison) immediately - dead DPS is zero DPS.
Interrupt assigned casts; coordinate kicks so you don't overlap and waste them.
Use AoE on packs and single-target on bosses; don't AoE before the tank has threat.

dungeonGeneral: Let the tank set the pace and the marks; follow the kill order.
Use crowd control on dangerous casters/healers in big pulls when the group lacks AoE.
Save Bloodlust/Heroism for the hardest boss or a tight enrage, not on trash.
Loot need only what your class/spec uses; greed the rest to keep groups happy.
On heroics, mechanics matter more than gear - know the 1-2 key abilities of each boss before pulling.
raidGeneral: Know your assignment before the pull: kill order, interrupt rotation, who soaks/kites/dispels.
Spread or stack exactly as the fight demands; many raid abilities punish the wrong one.
Don't stand in avoidable damage - 'don't stand in fire' is the #1 cause of avoidable wipes.
Stagger raid cooldowns across phases instead of stacking them all early.
Designate target-swap priority for adds so the raid focuses fire instantly.
questFlow: Group quests in the same zone/sub-zone and turn them in together to cut travel time.
Pick up every quest from a hub before leaving it; many chains unlock follow-ups at the same NPC.
Prefer kill+collect quests that share the same mobs/area so one pull progresses several objectives.
Track the breadcrumb quests that send you to the next zone so you arrive already on the chain.
Use flight paths and hearthstone resets to minimize backtracking between turn-ins.
highXpServer: On multiplied-XP realms you out-level zones fast: drop quests that have turned GRAY (trivial) - their XP is negligible.
Keep quests that are part of a CHAIN leading to a reward, a dungeon attunement, or the next zone's breadcrumb, even if low XP.
Skip long fetch/escort quests with heavy travel when their XP no longer moves your bar much.
After a few levels, abandon green quests whose objectives are now far below your level unless they give a useful item/reward.
Prioritize dense kill quests and dungeon quests: on high rates a single dungeon run can complete several at once.
When in doubt, compare the quest's level to yours: 5+ levels below you on a 5x realm is usually not worth the travel.
